scheme_details.scheme_name: National Scholarship Portal (NSP) - Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students
scheme_details.ministry: Ministry of Education, Government of India
scheme_details.objective: To provide financial assistance to meritorious students from economically weaker sections to meet a part of their day-to-day expenses while pursuing higher studies.

eligibility_criteria.academic_performance: Students who are above 80th percentile of successful candidates in the relevant stream from the respective Board of Examination in Class XII (10+2 pattern).
eligibility_criteria.course_type: Pursuing regular courses (not correspondence or distance mode) in College/Institutions recognized by AICTE, UGC, MCI, DCI, or other regulatory bodies.
eligibility_criteria.family_income_limit: Gross parental/family income should not exceed Rs. 4.5 Lakh per annum.
eligibility_criteria.other_scholarships: The applicant must not be availing the benefits of any other scholarship scheme (Centrally funded, State-funded, or UGC/AICTE).

financial_assistance_and_benefits.graduation_level: Rs. 12,000 per annum for the first three years of college/university courses.
financial_assistance_and_benefits.post_graduation_level: Rs. 20,000 per annum for post-graduate courses (or for 4th and 5th years in case of professional courses).
financial_assistance_and_benefits.technical_professional_courses: For 4-5 year professional courses like B.Tech/B.E/B.Arch, Rs. 20,000 per annum is provided in the 4th and 5th year.
financial_assistance_and_benefits.duration: Maximum duration of 5 years, subject to regular renewal based on maintaining minimum 50% marks and 75% attendance.

reservation_and_distribution.slots_allocation: Total of 82,000 fresh scholarships awarded every year (41,000 for boys and 41,000 for girls) distributed among State Education Boards based on the state's population in the 18-25 age group.

required_documents: Scanned copy of Class XII Marksheet
Income Certificate issued by the competent authority
Caste Certificate (if applying under SC/ST/OBC category)
Bank Account details linked with Aadhaar (for Direct Benefit Transfer)
Bonafide Student Certificate from the current College/Institute
Fee Receipt of the current academic year

application_process_steps: One-Time Registration (OTR) on the NSP Portal.
Filling up the specific Scholarship Application Form.
Submission of the form for Institutional Verification.
District/State-level re-verification before final merit list generation.
